title: Control Handoff After Harvest
focus_questions: Was this only a pause after harvest, or did control actually change hands?
Did the old winning side fail its first meaningful defense?

summary.thesis: After a visible objective is completed, the next high-value question is whether the old winner still controls the market or whether control has already transferred to the opposite side.
summary.operator_translation: Do not keep trading the completed campaign if the first real post-harvest defense already failed.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card when harvest is done and the replay starts showing first-defensive-failure or counter-side acceptance.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: a completed objective with emerging post-harvest transition. Required evidence: harvest completion, weakening continuation, and failed first defense or counter-side acceptance. Do not trade: if the market is only pausing or if control transfer is not yet proven. Real trigger: failed first meaningful defense after harvest. Failure point: old winner quickly reclaims and continues the same campaign.

when_relevant: A clear objective has already been completed.
Continuation after the objective is weakening.
The market must now decide whether the old winner still controls the tape.
required_evidence: harvest_completed or equivalent completion evidence exists
post_harvest behavior is visible rather than just assumed
the original winning side either fails defense or the opposite side gains acceptance
confirmation_signals: same-side continuation weakens soon after the objective is completed
the opposite side achieves real acceptance on the first meaningful counter move
the original winning side cannot defend the first important revisit after harvest
invalidation_signals: the old winner quickly resumes continuation after only a pause
the supposed handoff never produces counter-side acceptance
the first defense after harvest actually holds cleanly
no_trade_conditions: Do not force a control handoff read just because the market pauses after objective completion.
Do not call control transferred before the opposite side can hold acceptance away from the harvested area.
Do not keep pressing old-direction continuation if the old winner already failed the first meaningful defense.

entry_archetypes.name: failed first defense
entry_archetypes.description: Trade with the new side after the old winner fails the first meaningful post-harvest defense.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the market is still unresolved and only printing small counter moves.

entry_archetypes.name: counter-side acceptance after completion
entry_archetypes.description: Trade once the opposite side can hold acceptance away from the harvested zone.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if acceptance is not yet durable.

management_notes: This card is strongest when paired with post-harvest response instead of used in isolation.
The first failed defense after harvest matters more than random small pauses.
Once control truly hands off, old continuation logic should be downgraded quickly.
review_questions: What proved that control actually changed hands after the harvest?
Did the old winning side fail its first real defense, or only pause?
Was the operator still trading the completed campaign after the market had already changed script?
